addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
4040	   GetCostFixed (which ignores its context); both LZ77OptimalRun callers pass one
4041	   of them.  Constraining the pointer makes the indirect call resolvable, and a
4042	   fresh SymbolStats is a valid context for both. */
4043	__CPROVER_requires(costmodel == GetCostStat || costmodel == GetCostFixed)
4044	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(costcontext, sizeof(SymbolStats)))
4045	/* ---- dynamic-programming arrays ----
4046	   costs[] and length_array[] are each indexed in [0, inend - instart]; both are
4047	   allocated by the caller with (blocksize + 1) entries. */
4048	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(length_array,
4049	                                      (inend - instart + 1) * sizeof(*length_array)))
4050	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(costs,
4051	                                      (inend - instart + 1) * sizeof(*costs)))
4052	/* ---- path output handles ----
4053	   path/pathsize are valid handles.  The body frees *path before overwriting it,
4054	   so the incoming pointer must be a freeable value; NULL satisfies that and
4055	   matches the callers' fresh-or-reset path. */
4056	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(path, sizeof(*path)))
4057	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pathsize, sizeof(*pathsize)))
4058	__CPROVER_requires(*path == NULL)
4059	/* ---- hash object ----
4060	   The hash and all of its sub-arrays must be valid, writable objects, sized as
4061	   ZopfliAllocHash allocates them (65536-entry head tables, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE
4062	   per-slot arrays). */
4063	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
4064	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, (size_t)65536 * sizeof(*h->head)))
4065	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, (size_t)65536 * sizeof(*h->head2)))
4066	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev)))
4067	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->prev2)))
4068	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval)))
4069	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->hashval2)))
4070	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(*h->same)))
4071	/* ---- block state, longest-match cache, and output store ---- */
4072	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
4073	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
4074	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
4075	/* The cost returned is the cost-model cost to reach the end: non-negative and
4076	   below the sentinel (matching the closing assertion). */
4077	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value >= 0)
4078	__CPROVER_ensures(__CPROVER_return_value < ZOPFLI_LARGE_FLOAT)
4079	/* Mutates the path handles, the DP arrays, the hash, the cache slots, and the
4080	   output store (the union of the three callees' effects). */
4081	__CPROVER_assigns(*path, *pathsize,
4082	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(costs),
4083	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(length_array),
4084	                  h->val, h->val2,
4085	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head),
4086	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2),
4087	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev),
4088	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2),
4089	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval),
4090	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2),
4091	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(h->same),
4092	                  store->size,
4093	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->litlens),
4094	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->dists),
4095	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->pos),
4096	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->ll_symbol),
4097	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->d_symbol),
4098	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->ll_counts),
4099	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(store->d_counts),
4100	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(s->lmc->length),
4101	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(s->lmc->dist),
4102	                  __CPROVER_object_whole(s->lmc->sublen))
4103	{
4104	    double cost = GetBestLengths(s, in, instart, inend, costmodel,
4105	                                 costcontext, length_array, h, costs);
4106	    free(*path);
4107	    *path = 0;
4108	    *pathsize = 0;
4109	    TraceBackwards(inend - instart, length_array, path, pathsize);
4110	    FollowPath(s, in, instart, inend, *path, *pathsize, store, h);
4111	    assert(cost < ZOPFLI_LARGE_FLOAT);
4112	    return cost;
4113	}
4114	
4115	void ZopfliCleanHash(ZopfliHash *h)
4116	/* The hash struct and each of its seven array buffers must be valid, freshly
4117	   allocated objects so that the calls to free() are well defined. */
4118	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
4119	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, sizeof(*h->head)))
4120	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, sizeof(*h->prev)))
4121	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, sizeof(*h->hashval)))
4122	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, sizeof(*h->head2)))
4123	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, sizeof(*h->prev2)))
4124	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, sizeof(*h->hashval2)))
4125	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, sizeof(*h->same)))
4126	__CPROVER_assigns()
4127	__CPROVER_frees(h->head, h->prev, h->hashval, h->head2, h->prev2, h->hashval2,
4128	                h->same)
4129	{
4130	    free(h->head);
4131	    free(h->prev);
4132	    free(h->hashval);
4133	
4134	    free(h->head2);
4135	    free(h->prev2);
4136	    free(h->hashval2);
4137	
4138	    free(h->same);
4139	}
4140	
4141	void ZopfliAllocHash(size_t window_size, ZopfliHash *h)
4142	/* The hash object must be a valid, writable struct.  The function only writes
4143	   the seven array-pointer fields of *h; it never dereferences them, so no
4144	   constraints on their incoming values are needed.  window_size is bounded by
4145	   the (only) value callers pass, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE, which also keeps the
4146	   malloc size computations free of overflow. */
4147	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
4148	__CPROVER_requires(window_size <= ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
4149	__CPROVER_assigns(h->head, h->prev, h->hashval, h->same,
4150	                  h->head2, h->prev2, h->hashval2)
4151	{
4152	    h->head = (int *)malloc(sizeof(*h->head) * 65536);
4153	    h->prev = (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*h->prev) * window_size);
4154	    h->hashval = (int *)malloc(sizeof(*h->hashval) * window_size);
4155	
4156	    h->same = (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*h->same) * window_size);
4157	
4158	    h->head2 = (int *)malloc(sizeof(*h->head2) * 65536);
4159	    h->prev2 = (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(*h->prev2) * window_size);
4160	    h->hashval2 = (int *)malloc(sizeof(*h->hashval2) * window_size);
4161	}
4162	
4163	/*
4164	Does the same as ZopfliLZ77Optimal, but optimized for the fixed tree of the
4165	deflate standard.
4166	The fixed tree never gives the best compression. But this gives the best
4167	possible LZ77 encoding possible with the fixed tree.
4168	This does not create or output any fixed tree, only LZ77 data optimized for
4169	using with a fixed tree.
4170	If instart is larger than 0, it uses values before instart as starting
4171	dictionary.
4172	*/
4173	void ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed(ZopfliBlockState *s,
4174	                            const unsigned char *in,
4175	                            size_t instart, size_t inend,
4176	                            ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
4177	{
4178	    /* Dist to get to here with smallest cost. */
4179	    size_t blocksize = inend - instart;
4180	    unsigned short *length_array =
4181	        (unsigned short *)malloc(sizeof(unsigned short) * (blocksize + 1));
4182	    unsigned short *path = 0;
4183	    size_t pathsize = 0;
4184	    ZopfliHash hash;
4185	    ZopfliHash *h = &hash;
4186	    float *costs = (float *)malloc(sizeof(float) * (blocksize + 1));
4187	
4188	    if (!costs)
4189	        exit(-1); /* Allocation failed. */
4190	    if (!length_array)
4191	        exit(-1); /* Allocation failed. */
4192	
4193	    ZopfliAllocHash(ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE, h);
4194	
4195	    s->blockstart = instart;
4196	    s->blockend = inend;
4197	
4198	    /* Shortest path for fixed tree This one should give the shortest possible
4199	    result for fixed tree, no repeated runs are needed since the tree is known. */
4200	    LZ77OptimalRun(s, in, instart, inend, &path, &pathsize,
4201	                   length_array, GetCostFixed, 0, store, h, costs);
4202	
4203	    free(length_array);
4204	    free(path);
4205	    free(costs);
4206	    ZopfliCleanHash(h);
4207	}
4208	
4209	void ZopfliCleanCache(ZopfliLongestMatchCache *lmc)

content.text: The `ZopfliCleanHash` function already has specifications. Let me verify it.

`ZopfliCleanHash` verifies successfully (exit 0).

The function already had complete specifications in place:
- **`__CPROVER_requires`**: `h` and each of its seven array buffers (`head`, `prev`, `hashval`, `head2`, `prev2`, `hashval2`, `same`) must be fresh allocations, making the `free()` calls well-defined.
- **`__CPROVER_assigns()`**: empty — no field assignments.
- **`__CPROVER_frees(...)`**: declares the seven buffers as freed.

CBMC confirms these are sound: **`ZopfliCleanHash verified successfully`**.
